vocabularies.definition: An area outside a window where you can stand or sit.
vocabularies.example:
There are flowers on the balcony.
I like sitting on the balcony in the evening.
Our apartment has a small balcony.

vocabularies.definition: A room in a house that is under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
We keep our old toys in the basement.
It's dark down in the basement.
The washing machine is in the basement.

vocabularies.definition: The door or gate where you go into a place.
vocabularies.example:
Wait for me at the front entrance.
The entrance to the cave is small.
Where is the main entrance?

vocabularies.definition: A room at the top of a house under the roof.
vocabularies.example:
He stores his old books in the attic.
It's very dusty in the attic.
The stairs to the attic are narrow.

vocabularies.definition: The top part of a room that you look up at.
vocabularies.example:
The ceiling is painted white.
A large spider is on the ceiling.
There is a lamp hanging from the ceiling.

vocabularies.definition: A pipe on a roof that lets smoke from a fire go out.
vocabularies.example:
Smoke is coming out of the chimney.
Birds often build nests on the chimney.
Santa Claus comes down the chimney.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of cloth you can pull across a window.
vocabularies.example:
Please close the curtains; it's getting dark.
The curtains match the carpet.
She bought new blue curtains for the bedroom.

vocabularies.definition: A soft bag used for sitting or leaning on for comfort.
vocabularies.example:
There are some soft cushions on the sofa.
I like to lean on a cushion when I read.
She bought a colorful cushion for her chair.

vocabularies.definition: A box that slides into a desk or cabinet for keeping things in.
vocabularies.example:
The spoons are in the top drawer.
Keep your socks in the bottom drawer.
I can't open this drawer; it's stuck.

vocabularies.definition: A cold machine used to keep food and drinks fresh.
vocabularies.example:
Put the milk back in the fridge.
The fridge is full of vegetables.
Is there any juice in the fridge?

vocabularies.definition: A large, soft cover used on a bed to keep you warm.
vocabularies.example:
It's cold tonight, so I need an extra blanket.
She wrapped herself in a warm blanket.
Can I have another blanket, please?

vocabularies.definition: A thick material that covers the floor in a room.
vocabularies.example:
We have a soft red carpet in the living room.
Please take off your shoes; the carpet is clean.
He spilled some juice on the carpet.

vocabularies.definition: A thing that gives light to a room, usually put on a table or desk.
vocabularies.example:
Turn on the lamp so you can see better.
There is a lamp on my bedside table.
She bought a beautiful new lamp.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of glass where you can see yourself.
vocabularies.example:
She looked at herself in the mirror.
There is a large mirror in the bathroom.
The mirror is broken.

vocabularies.definition: The top cover of a building or house.
vocabularies.example:
The cat is sitting on the roof.
Heavy rain is hitting the roof.
They are repairing the roof of the house.

vocabularies.definition: A flat board on a wall or in a cupboard where you put books or objects.
vocabularies.example:
Put the books back on the shelf.
The shelf is full of old toys.
Can you reach the top shelf?

vocabularies.definition: Something you use with water to wash your hands and body.
vocabularies.example:
Wash your hands with soap and water.
This soap smells like lemons.
I need to buy some more soap.

vocabularies.definition: A bowl with a seat in the bathroom used for going to the bathroom.
vocabularies.example:
Where is the toilet, please?
The toilet is at the end of the hall.
Don't forget to flush the toilet.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of thick cloth used to dry your body after a shower.
vocabularies.example:
I need a dry towel after my shower.
The towel is hanging behind the door.
Please put the wet towel in the wash.

grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ vị trí vật dụng trong nhà.
grammars.example:
The keys are in the drawer.
The cat is under the balcony.

vocabularies.definition: A very large animal that lived millions of years ago, before people.
vocabularies.example:
Dinosaurs lived millions of years ago.
I saw a dinosaur skeleton at the museum.
Which is your favorite dinosaur?

vocabularies.definition: An insect with large, beautiful, colorful wings.
vocabularies.example:
A colorful butterfly sat on the flower.
Butterflies have beautiful wings.
I saw a butterfly in the garden.

vocabularies.definition: A sea animal with a soft body and eight long arms.
vocabularies.example:
An octopus has eight arms.
Octopuses live deep in the ocean.
The octopus hid under a rock.

vocabularies.definition: A large animal with a long neck and one or two humps on its back.
vocabularies.example:
Camels can live in the desert for a long time.
Have you ever ridden a camel?
Camels carry heavy things across the sand.

vocabularies.definition: An animal that looks like a small horse with long ears.
vocabularies.example:
The donkey carried the heavy baskets.
Donkeys are very strong animals.
I saw a donkey at the farm.

vocabularies.definition: A large, strong bird that hunts and eats smaller animals.
vocabularies.example:
The eagle flew high above the mountains.
Eagles have very sharp eyes.
An eagle can catch small animals.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with six legs like an ant or a fly.
vocabularies.example:
Bees and ants are types of insects.
There are many insects in the rainforest.
Study the insects with a magnifying glass.

vocabularies.definition: A large Australian animal that hops and carries its baby in a pouch.
vocabularies.example:
Kangaroos live in Australia.
A mother kangaroo carries its baby in a pouch.
Kangaroos can jump very high.

vocabularies.definition: A small reptile with short legs, a long tail, and rough skin.
vocabularies.example:
A small lizard is sunbathing on the wall.
Lizards have long tails.
Some lizards can change their color.

vocabularies.definition: A large, beautiful white bird with a long neck that lives on lakes.
vocabularies.example:
Look at the beautiful white swan on the lake.
Swans are very graceful birds.
The swan protect its nest.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal that looks like a mouse with wings and flies at night.
vocabularies.example:
Bats fly at night to find food.
I saw a bat in the cave.
Bats sleep hanging upside down.

vocabularies.definition: A large, strong wild animal with thick fur.
vocabularies.example:
Polar bears live in the cold Arctic.
The bear is looking for honey.
Don't go near the bear.

vocabularies.definition: A flying insect that makes honey and can sting you.
vocabularies.example:
Bees make honey from flowers.
A bee sting can be painful.
Bees are very busy insects.

vocabularies.definition: A large, dangerous reptile with sharp teeth that lives in rivers.
vocabularies.example:
Crocodiles have very sharp teeth.
Be careful near the river; there are crocodiles.
A crocodile can swim very fast.

vocabularies.definition: A very clever sea animal that is friendly and looks like a large fish.
vocabularies.example:
Dolphins are very intelligent animals.
We saw dolphins jumping in the ocean.
Dolphins like to play with people.

vocabularies.definition: A huge grey animal with large ears and a long trunk.
vocabularies.example:
An elephant has a long trunk.
Elephants are the largest land animals.
We saw an elephant at the zoo.

vocabularies.definition: A tall African animal with a very long neck and long legs.
vocabularies.example:
The giraffe has a very long neck.
Giraffes eat leaves from the tops of trees.
I saw a baby giraffe today.

vocabularies.definition: A brightly colored bird that can sometimes copy what people say.
vocabularies.example:
The parrot can repeat the words I say.
Parrots have very colorful feathers.
My grandmother has a pet parrot.

vocabularies.definition: A large, dangerous fish with very sharp teeth that lives in the ocean.
vocabularies.example:
Sharks have many rows of teeth.
We went diving and saw a shark.
Some sharks are very dangerous.

vocabularies.definition: A huge sea animal that looks like a giant fish but breathes air.
vocabularies.example:
The blue whale is the biggest animal in the world.
Whales breathe air through a blowhole.
We saw a whale from the boat.

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về khả năng của động vật.
grammars.example:
Kangaroos can jump high.
Penguins can't fly.

vocabularies.definition: The natural world of land, air, and water where people and animals live.
vocabularies.example:
We must protect the environment.
Plastic is bad for the environment.
We learn about the environment at school.

vocabularies.definition: Dirty or bad things in the air, water, or earth that make it not clean.
vocabularies.example:
Air pollution is a big problem in cities.
There is a lot of water pollution here.
Cars cause a lot of pollution.

vocabularies.definition: To put used paper, glass, or plastic into special bins so it can be made into new things.
vocabularies.example:
We should recycle glass and paper.
How do you recycle plastic bottles?
Recycling helps the planet.

vocabularies.definition: A very dry place with lots of sand and not much water or plants.
vocabularies.example:
It is very hot and dry in the desert.
Not many plants grow in the desert.
The Sahara is the largest desert in Africa.

vocabularies.definition: A huge area of salty water that covers a lot of the Earth.
vocabularies.example:
Whales live in the deep ocean.
The ocean is much bigger than a sea.
We shouldn't throw trash into the ocean.

vocabularies.definition: A very large, round object in space that moves around a bright star, like Earth.
vocabularies.example:
Earth is the planet we live on.
There are eight planets in our solar system.
We must keep our planet clean.

vocabularies.definition: A thick forest in a hot part of the world where it rains very often.
vocabularies.example:
Many animals live in the Amazon rainforest.
Rainforests are important for the Earth's air.
It rains a lot in the rainforest.

vocabularies.definition: The huge, dark area outside Earth where the stars and planets are.
vocabularies.example:
Astronauts travel to space.
Stars and planets are in space.
The moon is beautiful in space.

vocabularies.definition: Very bad weather with a lot of rain, strong winds, and sometimes thunder.
vocabularies.example:
There was a loud thunder during the storm.
The storm broke some trees in the garden.
Stay inside when there is a storm.

vocabularies.definition: A mountain with a hole in the top where hot liquid rock sometimes comes out.
vocabularies.example:
The volcano erupted last night.
Hot lava flows down from the volcano.
Be careful near the volcano.

vocabularies.definition: Something built over a river or a road so you can cross it.
vocabularies.example:
We drove across the bridge.
This bridge is very old.
The bridge connects the two sides of the river.

vocabularies.definition: A large hole in the side of a hill, mountain, or under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
The cave was dark and cold.
We explored the deep cave with flashlights.
Bats live inside the cave.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land covered with many trees and plants.
vocabularies.example:
There are many trees in the forest.
We went for a walk in the forest.
Many wild animals live in the forest.

vocabularies.definition: An area of high land that is smaller than a mountain.
vocabularies.example:
We climbed to the top of the hill.
The view from the hill is beautiful.
There is a small house on the hill.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of land that has water all around it.
vocabularies.example:
We stayed on a beautiful tropical island.
You need a boat to get to the island.
The island is surrounded by blue water.

vocabularies.definition: A very high and steep piece of land, much taller than a hill.
vocabularies.example:
Mount Everest is the highest mountain.
There is snow on top of the mountain.
We went skiing on the mountain.

vocabularies.definition: A huge area of salty water that covers a lot of the Earth.
vocabularies.example:
The ocean is full of fish and whales.
We swam in the warm ocean.
The ocean water is very salty.

vocabularies.definition: A narrow track on the ground for people to walk on in a garden or forest.
vocabularies.example:
Follow the path through the woods.
The path leads to the hidden garden.
Don't run on the muddy path.

vocabularies.definition: A long, moving stream of fresh water that goes into the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The river flows into the sea.
We went fishing in the river.
The river is very wide here.

vocabularies.definition: A large or small piece of hard stone.
vocabularies.example:
The climbers climbed up the steep rock.
He sat on a large rock by the lake.
There are many rocks on the beach.

vocabularies.definition: A place where a stream or river falls down from a high cliff or rock.
vocabularies.example:
The waterfall is very tall and beautiful.
We took a photo of the waterfall.
You can hear the sound of the waterfall from far away.

grammars.description: Dùng để giới thiệu các thành phần trong môi trường.
grammars.example:
There is a volcano on the island.
There are many stars in space.

vocabularies.definition: An event where people or teams try to win to be the best.
vocabularies.example:
He won first prize in the art competition.
Are you entering the swimming competition?
The competition was very difficult.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two teams hit a ball over a high net using their hands.
vocabularies.example:
We play volleyball on the beach.
I am in the school volleyball team.
She hit the volleyball over the net.

vocabularies.definition: A person or a team that wins a very important competition or game.
vocabularies.example:
She is the world chess champion.
Our team became the champions.
He wants to be a tennis champion.

vocabularies.definition: An exciting, unusual, and sometimes dangerous thing to do.
vocabularies.example:
Reading a book is like going on an adventure.
We had a big adventure in the mountains.
Are you ready for an adventure?

vocabularies.definition: To find and keep many things together as a hobby, like stamps or coins.
vocabularies.example:
My brother collects comic books.
I like to collect stickers.
We need to collect wood for the fire.

vocabularies.definition: The place where you kick or hit the ball to score points in games like football.
vocabularies.example:
He scored a fantastic goal in the final minute.
The crowd cheered when we scored a goal.
How many goals did he score?

vocabularies.definition: An activity that you enjoy doing in your free time.
vocabularies.example:
Painting is my favorite hobby.
Do you have any interesting hobbies?
My hobby is taking photos.

vocabularies.definition: A person who joins in playing a game or a sport.
vocabularies.example:
There are eleven players in a football team.
She is the best tennis player in the school.
The players were tired after the match.

vocabularies.definition: A large, open building with seats around a playing field for sports.
vocabularies.example:
The stadium was full of excited fans.
We went to the stadium to watch the match.
They build a huge stadium for the Olympics.

vocabularies.definition: A group of people who play a sport or game together against others.
vocabularies.example:
Our school team won the trophy.
I like working in a team.
Who is the captain of your team?

vocabularies.definition: An indoor game played by moving pieces on a special board, like chess.
vocabularies.example:
We like playing board games on rainy days.
My favorite board game is Monopoly.
Let's play a board game tonight.

vocabularies.definition: A board game for two players where you move pieces to trap the other's king.
vocabularies.example:
He is very good at playing chess.
I want to learn how to play chess.
My father taught me chess when I was six.

vocabularies.definition: A word game where you guess words from clues and write them into empty squares.
vocabularies.example:
He enjoys doing the crossword in the newspaper.
This crossword is very difficult.
Can you help me with this crossword clue?

vocabularies.definition: A person who really likes or supports a sports team or a famous person.
vocabularies.example:
He is a big fan of football.
The fans cheered loudly for their team.
I am a fan of that singer.

vocabularies.definition: A game or sports competition between two people or two teams.
vocabularies.example:
Did you watch the football match last night?
The match was very exciting.
Our team won the final match.

vocabularies.definition: Something you are given for winning a game or doing something very well.
vocabularies.example:
She won a prize for her beautiful painting.
The first prize is a new bike.
He was very happy with his prize.

vocabularies.definition: A fast competition to see who can run, drive, or swim the quickest.
vocabularies.example:
I'm going to run in the school race.
Who won the car race?
The race was very fast and exciting.

vocabularies.definition: To move on ice wearing special shoes with metal blades, or shoes with wheels.
vocabularies.example:
We like to go to the ice rink to skate.
Can you skate on the lake?
He is learning how to skate.

vocabularies.definition: The sport of sliding down snow-covered hills on a pair of long, flat boards.
vocabularies.example:
We go skiing in the mountains every winter.
Skiing is a very exciting sport.
Have you ever been skiing?

vocabularies.definition: A single wide board that you stand on to slide down a snow mountain.
vocabularies.example:
He wants to learn how to snowboard.
Snowboarding is more difficult than skiing for me.
He bought a new snowboard.

grammars.description: Dùng để kể về các sự kiện thể thao trong quá khứ.
grammars.example:
He won the match yesterday.
We played volleyball last weekend.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling afraid, scared, or nervous because of something bad.
vocabularies.example:
She is frightened of spiders.
Don't be frightened, the dog is friendly.
I was frightened by the loud noise.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling sad because something was not as good as you expected.
vocabularies.example:
He was disappointed with his exam results.
We were disappointed that it rained.
She felt disappointed when she lost.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling very happy and enthusiastic because something good is happening.
vocabularies.example:
The children are excited about the trip.
I'm so excited to see you!
He was too excited to sleep.

vocabularies.definition: Not afraid to do dangerous or difficult things.
vocabularies.example:
The firefighters were very brave.
Be brave; it won't hurt much.
He is a brave knight.

vocabularies.definition: Smart and quick to learn or understand things.
vocabularies.example:
She is very clever at math.
That was a clever idea!
The dog is clever enough to open the door.

vocabularies.definition: Known or recognized by many people around the world.
vocabularies.example:
She is a famous singer.
This castle is famous all over the world.
I want to be famous one day.

vocabularies.definition: Kind, pleasant, and acting like a good friend to others.
vocabularies.example:
The local people are very friendly.
He has a friendly face.
Our neighbor is very friendly to us.

vocabularies.definition: Making you smile or laugh.
vocabularies.example:
That was a funny story!
He is the funniest person in our class.
The movie was very funny.

vocabularies.definition: Caring about other people and wanting to help them.
vocabularies.example:
Thank you for your kind words.
She is a kind and helpful teacher.
Be kind to animals.

vocabularies.definition: Unusual or surprising in a way that is hard to understand.
vocabularies.example:
I heard a strange noise in the garden.
That's a strange thing to say.
He has a strange hobby.

vocabularies.definition: To think that something is true or that someone is telling the truth.
vocabularies.example:
I believe that everything will be okay.
Do you believe in ghosts?
I can't believe it's already February.

vocabularies.definition: Not interesting or exciting, making you feel tired of it.
vocabularies.example:
The movie was very boring; I nearly fell asleep.
I find history a bit boring.
Doing the same thing every day is boring.

vocabularies.definition: Giving a lot of attention to what you are doing so you do not make a mistake.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful when you cross the road.
You should be more careful with your toys.
He is a very careful driver.

vocabularies.definition: All through a period of time, or at some point in a period of time.
vocabularies.example:
It rained during the night.
Please be quiet during the exam.
We stayed at home during the storm.

vocabularies.definition: To make something easy to understand by talking clearly about it.
vocabularies.example:
Can you explain this math problem to me?
The teacher explained the rules of the game.
It's difficult to explain how I feel.

vocabularies.definition: To try to give an answer when you are not sure if it is right.
vocabularies.example:
Can you guess what I have in my bag?
I guess he is about ten years old.
Guess who I saw today!

vocabularies.definition: To take place or to occur, usually without being planned.
vocabularies.example:
What happened to your leg?
A funny thing happened at school today.
When did the accident happen?

vocabularies.definition: To want something good to happen and think that it might happen.
vocabularies.example:
I hope you have a great birthday.
I hope the weather will be sunny tomorrow.
We hope to see you again soon.

vocabularies.definition: To have a certain meaning. Also, not kind or nice to other people.
vocabularies.example:
What does this word mean?
Don't be mean to your little sister.
I didn't mean to hurt your feelings.

vocabularies.definition: To like one thing or person better than another.
vocabularies.example:
I prefer tea to coffee.
Which do you prefer, apples or oranges?
I prefer staying at home on rainy days.

grammars.description: Một số tính từ đi kèm giới từ cố định.
grammars.example:
I am frightened of snakes.
She is excited about the party.

vocabularies.definition: The sister of your mother or father, or the wife of your uncle.
vocabularies.example:
My aunt lives in London.
My aunt bought me a new dress.
Is your aunt coming to the party?

vocabularies.definition: The brother of your mother or father, or the husband of your aunt.
vocabularies.example:
My uncle is a pilot.
My uncle takes us fishing.
I visited my uncle last summer.

vocabularies.definition: The child of your aunt and uncle.
vocabularies.example:
I have many cousins.
My cousin is the same age as me.
We visited our cousins in the city.

vocabularies.definition: The man that a woman is married to.
vocabularies.example:
Her husband is a doctor.
My aunt's husband is my uncle.
Is he your husband?

vocabularies.definition: The woman that a man is married to.
vocabularies.example:
His wife is a teacher.
My uncle's wife is my aunt.
They have been husband and wife for ten years.

vocabularies.definition: A boy or man in relation to his parents.
vocabularies.example:
They have two sons and one daughter.
Their son is very good at football.
Is he your son?

vocabularies.definition: A girl or woman in relation to her parents.
vocabularies.example:
My daughter is six years old.
She is the youngest daughter in the family.
Their daughter loves drawing.

vocabularies.definition: The child of your son or daughter.
vocabularies.example:
They have one grandchild.
She loves spending time with her grandchildren.
Who is your grandchild?

vocabularies.definition: The parents of your mother or father.
vocabularies.example:
I visit my grandparents every weekend.
My grandparents live in a small house.
They are very kind grandparents.

vocabularies.definition: A person who lives next to you or near you in your street.
vocabularies.example:
Our neighbors are very noisy.
She is a very friendly neighbor.
I borrowed some sugar from my neighbor.

vocabularies.definition: The one friend that you like the most out of all your friends.
vocabularies.example:
Sarah has been my best friend since kindergarten.
We tell each other everything.
My best friend lives just across the street.

vocabularies.definition: The daughter of your son or daughter.
vocabularies.example:
They have a beautiful three-year-old granddaughter.
Her granddaughter visits her every Sunday.
She is very proud of her granddaughter.

vocabularies.definition: The son of your son or daughter.
vocabularies.example:
My grandson is starting school next week.
He loves playing football with his grandson.
Their grandson is very tall.

vocabularies.definition: Having a wife or husband.
vocabularies.example:
My aunt and uncle have been married for twenty years.
When did they get married?
They are a very happy married couple.

vocabularies.definition: A person you play a game with, work with, or live with.
vocabularies.example:
Who is your dancing partner for the competition?
He is my business partner.
Find a partner for the next activity.

vocabularies.definition: A man, woman, or child (plural is people).
vocabularies.example:
Who is that person over there?
She is a very kind and helpful person.
Only one person is allowed in the room at a time.

vocabularies.definition: Not married or not having a romantic partner.
vocabularies.example:
Is your brother married or single?
He is single and lives in a small apartment.
She enjoys being single.

vocabularies.definition: Your family name that comes after your first name.
vocabularies.example:
What is your surname, please?
My surname is Smith.
Write your first name and your surname here.

vocabularies.definition: A young person between 13 and 19 years old.
vocabularies.example:
The club is for teenagers aged 13 to 18.
He is a typical teenager who loves music.
There were many teenagers at the concert.

vocabularies.definition: One of two children born to the same mother at the same time.
vocabularies.example:
My sister has twin boys.
They are identical twins.
He has a twin brother.

grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ sự sở hữu giữa các thành viên gia đình.
grammars.example:
This is my aunt's house.
Those are my cousin's toys.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in your back.
vocabularies.example:
He has a terrible backache from lifting heavy boxes.
Sit straight or you will get a backache.
My father has a backache today.

vocabularies.definition: An illness where you make loud, harsh noises from your throat.
vocabularies.example:
She has a bad cough.
Cover your mouth when you cough.
He drank some tea for his cough.

vocabularies.definition: A pain inside your ear.
vocabularies.example:
I can't go swimming because I have an earache.

There is a cat on the roof.
There are many books on the shelf.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people can study after they leave high school.
vocabularies.example:
She is going to college next year.
He teaches at a local college.
The college has a large library.

vocabularies.definition: An official document you get when you finish university.
vocabularies.example:
He has a degree in history.
She is studying for a science degree.
A university degree is very important.

vocabularies.definition: A document showing that you have completed a course of study.
vocabularies.example:
She received her diploma at the ceremony.
He has a diploma in computer science.
Work hard to get your diploma.

vocabularies.definition: A high-level teacher at a university or college.
vocabularies.example:
The professor is giving a lecture.
She is a biology professor.
He wants to be a university professor.

vocabularies.definition: Money given to a student to help pay for their studies.
vocabularies.example:
She won a scholarship to study abroad.
He applied for a music scholarship.
Scholarships help students with their fees.

vocabularies.definition: One of the periods of time that the school year is divided into.
vocabularies.example:
The school term ends in December.
We have a mid-term break.
How many terms are there in a year?

vocabularies.definition: A piece of work that a student is asked to do.
vocabularies.example:
I have a math assignment for tomorrow.
The teacher gave us a fun assignment.
Submit your assignment on time.

vocabularies.definition: The act of being present at a place, like school or a meeting.
vocabularies.example:
Good attendance is important for learning.
The teacher marks the attendance every morning.
His attendance has improved this term.

vocabularies.definition: An official document that says something is true or has been achieved.
vocabularies.example:
She got a certificate for perfect attendance.
He showed his English certificate to the company.
The certificate is framed on the wall.

vocabularies.definition: A series of lessons in a particular subject.
vocabularies.example:
I am taking a cooking course.
The course lasts for six months.
Is the course difficult?

vocabularies.definition: The subjects that are taught in a school or college.
vocabularies.example:
Science is part of the school curriculum.
The curriculum is updated every few years.
What's in the history curriculum?

vocabularies.definition: A department or group of related subjects in a university.
vocabularies.example:
He is a member of the science faculty.
The university has several faculties.
She works for the faculty of arts.

vocabularies.definition: To complete a degree or a course of study.
vocabularies.example:
He will graduate from university next summer.
She is a high school graduate.
The graduation ceremony was very special.

vocabularies.definition: A talk given to a group of people to teach them about a subject.
vocabularies.example:
The professor gave an interesting lecture.
I am attending a lecture on history.
Take notes during the lecture.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can borrow books or study quietly.
vocabularies.example:
I like studying in the quiet library.
The library has thousands of books.
Please return the books to the library.

vocabularies.definition: A book with empty pages for writing notes in.
vocabularies.example:
Write the lesson in your notebook.
I have a new blue notebook.
Don't lose your notebook!

vocabularies.definition: A small bag or box for keeping pens and pencils in.
vocabularies.example:
Keep your pens and pencils in your pencase.
I have a colorful pencase.
Is this your pencase?

vocabularies.definition: A piece of work that involves careful study and planning.
vocabularies.example:
We are working on a group project.
The project is about the solar system.
When is the project due?

vocabularies.definition: A short test to check what you know.
vocabularies.example:
We had a surprise math quiz today.
Did you pass the quiz?
I need to study for the science quiz.

vocabularies.definition: The act of putting a name on an official list.
vocabularies.example:
Registration for the course starts next week.
Fill out the registration form.
Where is the registration desk?

vocabularies.definition: A detailed study of a subject to find out new information.
vocabularies.example:
He is doing research on ancient Egypt.
Research is important for the project.
Search for information during your research.

vocabularies.definition: One of the two periods of time that a school year is divided into.
vocabularies.example:
The first semester is almost over.
We have five subjects this semester.
I hope to get good grades this semester.

vocabularies.definition: The people who work for an organization, like a school.
vocabularies.example:
The school staff are very helpful.
He is a member of the teaching staff.
Meet the staff in the office.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is studying at a school or university.
vocabularies.example:
She is a very hardworking student.
There are thirty students in our class.
Welcome the new student.

vocabularies.definition: A teacher who teaches a small group of students or one student.
vocabularies.example:
He has a private tutor for math.
She works as an English tutor.
The tutor helped him understand the lesson.

grammars.structure: Prepositions of place: At school, In class
grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ địa điểm trong giáo dục.
grammars.example:
He is at school now.
She is in class.

vocabularies.definition: Used to change the subject or to finish a conversation.
vocabularies.example:
It's raining, but I'm going out anyway.
Thanks for your help anyway.
Anyway, let's get back to work.

vocabularies.definition: In addition to what has already been mentioned; different.
vocabularies.example:
Would you like anything else?
Who else is coming to the party?
Find someone else to help you.

vocabularies.definition: As much as is needed or wanted.
vocabularies.example:
Is there enough room for me?
He is tall enough to reach the shelf.
I've had enough of this weather.

vocabularies.definition: In place of something or someone else.
vocabularies.example:
I'll have tea instead of coffee.
Can we go tomorrow instead?
He stayed home instead of going to the park.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say 'yes' or to show that something is obvious.
vocabularies.example:
Of course you can come!
Can I borrow your pen? Of course.
Of course it's true.

vocabularies.definition: One time only.
vocabularies.example:
I've been there once before.
I'll call you once I arrive.
Once upon a time, there was a king.

vocabularies.definition: Used when you are not sure if something is true; maybe.
vocabularies.example:
Perhaps it will rain today.
Perhaps he forgot about our meeting.
May I join you? Perhaps.

vocabularies.definition: Used to show that something is true to a certain level; fairly.
vocabularies.example:
The movie was quite good.
I'm quite tired tonight.
It's quite a long way to the city.

vocabularies.definition: Used to show a preference or to mean 'fairly/quite'.
vocabularies.example:
It's rather cold today.
I'd rather stay at home than go out.
That's a rather strange question.

vocabularies.definition: In a short period of time from now.
vocabularies.example:
I'll be home soon.
See you soon!
The bus is coming soon.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is continuing to happen.
vocabularies.example:
Are you still here?
It is still raining.
He is still sleeping.

vocabularies.definition: With each other; in one group.
vocabularies.example:
Let's work together.
They went to the cinema together.
We can solve this problem together.

vocabularies.definition: In a good or successful way.
vocabularies.example:
He plays the piano very well.
I hope you are well.
Well, I should be going now.

vocabularies.definition: Until now; used in negative sentences and questions.
vocabularies.example:
I haven't seen the movie yet.
Is dinner ready yet?
No, not yet.

vocabularies.definition: Before now or before a particular time.
vocabularies.example:
I've already finished my homework.
Has he left already?
It's already 10 o'clock.

vocabularies.definition: In addition; too.
vocabularies.example:
She is a teacher, and also a writer.
I'm also coming to the party.
He also likes football.

vocabularies.definition: Used to show that something is surprising or unexpected.
vocabularies.example:
Even a child could understand this.
It's cold, even in the house.
He didn't even say goodbye.

vocabularies.definition: A short time ago; only; exactly.
vocabularies.example:
I've just arrived home.
It's just a small problem.
He is just a boy.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is possible; perhaps.
vocabularies.example:
Maybe we can go tomorrow.
Maybe she is busy.
Just maybe it will work.

vocabularies.definition: Not at any time; not once.
vocabularies.example:
I have never been to London.
He never tells lies.
Never give up!

vocabularies.definition: Many times; frequently.
vocabularies.example:
I often go for a walk in the evening.
How often do you go to the gym?
She often visits her grandparents.

vocabularies.definition: No more than; just one.
vocabularies.example:
I have only one brother.
It's only five o'clock.
He is the only child.

vocabularies.definition: Occasionally; not all the time.
vocabularies.example:
I sometimes stay up late.
Sometimes I feel a bit sad.
We sometimes go to the beach.

vocabularies.definition: Normally; what happens most of the time.
vocabularies.example:
I usually have breakfast at 7 am.
What do you usually do on Sundays?
She usually wears glasses.

vocabularies.definition: Nearly; not quite.
vocabularies.example:
The movie is almost over.
I've almost finished my work.
He is almost as tall as me.

grammars.description: Vị trí của trạng từ chỉ tần suất trong câu.
grammars.example:
I usually wake up early.
He is never late.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is good at sports and takes part in competitions.
vocabularies.example:
The athletes are training for the Olympics.
She is a very talented young athlete.
He wants to be a professional athlete.

vocabularies.definition: A competition to find out who is the best at a sport.
vocabularies.example:
They won the world championship last year.
The championship will be held in London.
Who is your favorite team in the championship?

vocabularies.definition: A person who trains a sports player or a team.
vocabularies.example:
The coach gave the players some advice.
She is an excellent swimming coach.
He was a football coach for twenty years.

vocabularies.definition: An event in which people try to win by being better than others.
vocabularies.example:
There is a spelling competition tomorrow.
She won first prize in the competition.
Competition is good for students.

vocabularies.definition: The set of tools or things needed for a particular activity.
vocabularies.example:
You need the right equipment for scuba diving.
The gym has new sports equipment.
Don't forget to pack your sports equipment.

vocabularies.definition: A room or building with equipment for doing physical exercise.
vocabularies.example:
I go to the gym three times a week.
The school has a very large gym.
He is working out at the gym.

vocabularies.definition: A sports game where two people or teams compete against each other.
vocabularies.example:
We watched an exciting football match.
The match ended in a draw.
Are you going to the match tonight?

vocabularies.definition: A small flat piece of metal given as a prize in a competition.
vocabularies.example:
He won a gold medal in the race.
She showed her medals to her parents.
The medal is made of solid gold.

vocabularies.definition: A person who takes part in a game or sport.
vocabularies.example:
He is the best player on the team.
There are eleven players in a football team.
She is a professional tennis player.

vocabularies.definition: A competition to see who is the fastest.
vocabularies.example:
I ran in the 100-meter race.
Who won the race?
The horses are ready for the race.

vocabularies.definition: The number of points a person or team gets in a game.
vocabularies.example:
What's the final score?
He scored a goal in the last minute.
Keep track of the score.

vocabularies.definition: A large open area with seats around it for watching sports.
vocabularies.example:
The stadium was full of fans.
They are building a new stadium in the city.
The stadium has sixty thousand seats.

vocabularies.definition: A prize, like a large silver cup, given to the winner of a competition.
vocabularies.example:
The team proudly held the trophy.
He has many trophies on his shelf.
Winning the trophy was his dream.

vocabularies.definition: A sport played with rackets and a shuttlecock over a high net.
vocabularies.example:
We play badminton in the garden.
Badminton is a fun and fast sport.
Do you have a badminton racket?

vocabularies.definition: A sport where two teams try to throw a ball through a high net.
vocabularies.example:
He is very tall, so he is good at basketball.
They play basketball every afternoon.
I love watching basketball games.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of riding a bicycle.
vocabularies.example:
Cycling is a great way to stay fit.
We went cycling in the country.
He is a professional cycling champion.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of catching fish for sport or for food.
vocabularies.example:
My grandfather loves going fishing.
We caught three fish on our fishing trip.
Fishing is very relaxing.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's goal.
vocabularies.example:
Football is the most popular sport in the world.
He plays football for his school.
Let's go and play football!

vocabularies.definition: A game played on grass where you hit a small ball into holes with a club.
vocabularies.example:
My uncle plays golf every weekend.
Golf is played on a large green course.
He is learning how to play golf.

vocabularies.definition: A game played on ice or grass with sticks and a puck or ball.
vocabularies.example:
She plays hockey for the national team.
Hockey is a fast and exciting sport.
Wear a helmet when playing hockey.

vocabularies.definition: A game played with an oval ball that can be kicked or carried.
vocabularies.example:
Rugby is a very tough sport.
They are watching a rugby match on TV.
He plays rugby for his college.

vocabularies.definition: The sport or activity of traveling in a boat with sails.
vocabularies.example:
We went sailing on the lake.
Sailing can be very peaceful.
Do you know how to go sailing?

vocabularies.definition: The sport of moving over snow on long, flat pieces of wood or plastic.
vocabularies.example:
We go skiing in the mountains every winter.
Skiing is a fun winter sport.
Be careful when you go skiing.

vocabularies.definition: The sport or activity of moving through water using your body.
vocabularies.example:
Swimming is good for your whole body.
She goes swimming every morning.
I love swimming in the ocean.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two or four people hit a ball over a net with rackets.
vocabularies.example:
He is playing a tennis match today.
Tennis is a very popular sport.
She has a new tennis racket.

grammars.description: Cách dùng các động từ đi kèm với các môn thể thao khác nhau.
grammars.example: Play football, Go swimming, Do gymnastics.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling fear or being scared of something.
vocabularies.example:
I am afraid of the dark.
Don't be afraid!
She is afraid of making mistakes.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling strong negative emotions because something bad happened.
vocabularies.example:
He was very angry with me.
Don't make your mother angry.
What are you angry about?

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy because you have nothing interesting to do.
vocabularies.example:
I'm bored; let's do something fun.
He looks bored with his book.
Are you bored yet?

vocabularies.definition: Giving a lot of attention to what you are doing to avoid danger.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful when you cross the road.
She is a very careful driver.
Carry that glass carefully.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling very happy and enthusiastic about something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm so excited about the holiday!
The children are excited for Christmas.
He gave an excited shout.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling afraid or scared.
vocabularies.example:
She was frightened by the big dog.
I was frightened of the loud thunder.
Don't be frightened; I'm here.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling pleased or glad about something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm so happy today!
She has a happy family.
Happy birthday!

vocabularies.definition: Wanting to know more about something.
vocabularies.example:
He is interested in ancient history.
Are you interested in photography?
I'm very interested in what you said.

vocabularies.definition: Being caring, helpful, and friendly to others.
vocabularies.example:
He is a very kind man.
Thank you for your kind help.
Be kind to animals.

vocabularies.definition: Having good things happen by chance.
vocabularies.example:
I am lucky to have such good friends.
You are very lucky to win the prize.
It was a lucky day.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy or sorry.
vocabularies.example:
I feel a bit sad today.
It's a very sad movie.
Why do you look so sad?

vocabularies.definition: Feeling wonder or shock because something unexpected happened.
vocabularies.example:
I was very surprised to see him.
She looked surprised at the news.
We were surprised by the party.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling that you need to rest or sleep.
vocabularies.example:
I'm very tired after the long walk.
You look tired; you should go to bed.
I'm tired of waiting.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy because you are thinking about problems.
vocabularies.example:
I'm worried about my exams.
Don't be worried; everything will be fine.
She had a worried look on her face.

vocabularies.definition: Being ready to face danger or pain without fear.
vocabularies.example:
The firefighter was very brave.
She was brave to tell the truth.
He is a brave boy.

vocabularies.definition: Being quick at learning and understanding things.
vocabularies.example:
She is a very clever student.
He is clever at fixing things.
That was a clever idea.

vocabularies.definition: Being known by many people.
vocabularies.example:
He is a famous actor.
She wants to be a famous singer.

vocabularies.definition: Being kind and pleasant to other people.
vocabularies.example:
The local people are very friendly.
He has a friendly face.
It was a friendly game.

vocabularies.definition: Having a big effect or being worth a lot of attention.
vocabularies.example:
It is important to study hard.
This is an important meeting.
Health is more important than money.

vocabularies.definition: Not wanting to work or make an effort.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be lazy; help me clean the room!
He is a lazy cat.
I feel a bit lazy today.

vocabularies.definition: Making a lot of loud, unpleasant noise.
vocabularies.example:
The neighbors are being very noisy.
It's a noisy street.
The machine is noisy.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling very pleased about something you have done.
vocabularies.example:
I am very proud of you.
He is proud of his country.
She was proud to win the prize.

vocabularies.definition: Having a lot of money or valuable things.
vocabularies.example:
The king was very rich.
She lives in a rich neighborhood.
He wants to be rich and famous.

vocabularies.definition: Unusual, surprising, or difficult to understand.
vocabularies.example:
That's a strange noise.
I had a strange dream.
He is a bit strange.

vocabularies.definition: Very good, pleasant, or enjoyable.
vocabularies.example:
We had a wonderful holiday.
The view is wonderful.
She is a wonderful person.

grammars.description: Một số tính từ chỉ cảm xúc đi kèm giới từ.
grammars.example: Afraid of, Excited about, Interested in.

vocabularies.definition: A structure built over a river or road so that people can cross it.
vocabularies.example:
We drove across the long bridge.
The bridge connects the two cities.
There is a wooden bridge over the river.

vocabularies.definition: A large strong building with high walls, built in the past for safety.
vocabularies.example:
The king lives in a beautiful castle.
I saw an old castle on the hill.
The castle has very thick walls.

vocabularies.definition: The place where two roads or walls meet.
vocabularies.example:
The shop is on the corner of the street.
Wait for me at the corner.
He is sitting in the corner of the room.

vocabularies.definition: The place where someone or something is going.
vocabularies.example:
We reached our destination at midnight.

The beach is a popular holiday destination.

vocabularies.definition: A building where large amounts of goods are made by machines.
vocabularies.example:
My dad works in a car factory.
The factory produces thousands of toys.
Don't go near the factory.

vocabularies.definition: A building where people can look at interesting and old things.
vocabularies.example:
We visited the natural history museum.
The museum is closed on Mondays.
There are many interesting things in the museum.

vocabularies.definition: A room or building where people work at desks.
vocabularies.example:
She works in a busy office.
Where is your office located?
I have some work to do at the office.

vocabularies.definition: The office of the local police force.
vocabularies.example:
The police station is near the bank.
He went to the police station to report the loss.
There are many police cars at the station.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can buy stamps and send letters.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the post office to sent a letter.
Where is the nearest post office?
The post office opens at 9 am.

vocabularies.definition: A place where trains stop for people to get on or off.
vocabularies.example:

The railway station was very crowded.

vocabularies.definition: A very tall building in a city.
vocabularies.example:
The city has many tall skyscrapers.
Look at that huge skyscraper!
You can see the whole city from the skyscraper.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you can go to watch a play or a show.
vocabularies.example:
We went to the theatre to see a play.
The theatre was beautifully decorated.
I love going to the theatre.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people go to study for a degree after high school.
vocabularies.example:
She is studying medicine at university.
He wants to go to university after school.
The university has a large campus.

vocabularies.definition: A small group of houses in the countryside, smaller than a town.
vocabularies.example:
He lives in a small village in the country.
The village is very quiet and peaceful.
I love visiting my grandparents' village.

vocabularies.definition: A road, path, or route to a particular place.
vocabularies.example:

This is the best way to learn English.
I don't know the way to the park.

vocabularies.definition: A shop where bread and cakes are made and sold.
vocabularies.example:
The bread in this bakery is delicious.
I bought some cakes at the bakery.
The bakery smells wonderful.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people keep their money safely.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the bank to get some money.
The bank is located in the city center.
He works for a big international bank.

vocabularies.definition: A shop where you can buy medicine and beauty products.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the chemist to buy some medicine.
Is there a chemist near here?
The chemist recommended this cream.

vocabularies.definition: A large shop divided into several parts selling different things.
vocabularies.example:
We went shopping at the department store.
The department store has ten floors.
You can buy anything in a department store.

vocabularies.definition: A building where fire engines are kept and firefighters work.
vocabularies.example:
The fire station is just down the street.

Firefighters work at the fire station.

vocabularies.definition: A place where cars are repaired or kept.
vocabularies.example:
I took my car to the garage for repair.
He works as a mechanic in a garage.
The garage is very busy today.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you pay to stay in a room and have meals.
vocabularies.example:
We are staying at a luxury hotel.
The hotel has a swimming pool.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people meet to buy and sell food and other goods.
vocabularies.example:
I buy fresh vegetables at the market.

Let's go to the market together.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can buy and eat a meal.
vocabularies.example:
We had a delicious meal at the restaurant.

I'd like to book a table at the restaurant.

vocabularies.definition: A large open area with seats around it for watching sports.
vocabularies.example:
The stadium was full of exciting fans.
They are playing football in the stadium.
The stadium is very impressive.

grammars.structure: Giving directions: Turn left, Go straight
grammars.description: Cách chỉ đường cơ bản.
grammars.example:
Go straight then turn left at the corner.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle used for taking sick or injured people to hospital.
vocabularies.example:

You can hear the siren of the ambulance.

vocabularies.definition: A time that you have arranged to see someone, like a doctor.

See you later at the party.
See you later, alligator!

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone success in something they are doing.
vocabularies.example:
Good luck with your exams!
I wish you good luck.
Good luck to everyone.

vocabularies.definition: Used for praising someone who has done something well.
vocabularies.example:
Well done on passing the test!
Well done, everyone!
That was well done.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to hurry or to be more reasonable.
vocabularies.example:
Come on, we're late!
Come on, you can do it!
Oh, come on, don't be sad.

vocabularies.definition: Used for warning someone that they are in danger.
vocabularies.example:
Look out! There's a car.
Look out for the wet floor.
Look out behind you!

vocabularies.definition: Used for warning someone of a danger or problem.
vocabularies.example:
Watch out for the big dog!
Watch out, it's hot.
Watch out for pickpockets.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to avoid danger or mistakes.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful with that knife.
Be careful when you cross the road.
Be careful what you say.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone that they should not be upset.
vocabularies.example:
Don't worry, be happy.
Don't worry about me.
Don't worry, everything is fine.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to go or act more quickly.
vocabularies.example:
Hurry up or we'll miss the train.
Hurry up and get ready.
Hurry up, please.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to move or work more slowly.
vocabularies.example:
Slow down, you're driving too fast.
Slow down and think.
Can you slow down, please?

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a quick recovery from being sick.
vocabularies.example:
I heard you're sick. Get well soon!
Get well soon, my friend.
We all hope you get well soon.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a happy day on their birthday.
vocabularies.example:
Happy birthday to you!
Have a very happy birthday.
I sent her a happy birthday card.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a happy Christmas.
vocabularies.example:
Merry Christmas to all!
I wish you a Merry Christmas.
We had a Merry Christmas together.

vocabularies.definition: Used for making a suggestion.
vocabularies.example:
How about going to the park?
How about a cup of tea?
How about tomorrow?

vocabularies.definition: Used for asking someone what is wrong.
vocabularies.example:
You look sad. What's the matter?
What's the matter with your arm?
Is anything the matter?

vocabularies.definition: Used for offering something to someone politely.
vocabularies.example:
Would you like some cake?
Would you like to come with us?
What would you like to drink?

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you believe something is true.
vocabularies.example:
Is he coming? - I think so.
I think so, but I'm not sure.
Yes, I think so too.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you want something NOT to happen.
vocabularies.example:
Will it rain? - I hope not.
I hope not for your sake.
Is he angry? - I hope not.

grammars.description: Các cách diễn đạt thông dụng trong giao tiếp hàng ngày.
grammars.example: You're welcome. See you later. Good luck.

vocabularies.definition: Any person; it does not matter who.
vocabularies.example:
Does anyone know the answer?
Anyone can do it.
I didn't tell anyone.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is not known or named.
vocabularies.example:
Someone is knocking at the door.
I need someone to help me.
Someone told me a secret.

vocabularies.definition: Not any person; nobody.
vocabularies.example:
No one knows where he is.
No one was home.
I saw no one.

vocabularies.definition: All the people in a group.
vocabularies.example:
Everyone is happy.
Hello, everyone!
Everyone needs a friend.

vocabularies.definition: Any object, event, or situation.
vocabularies.example:
Do you need anything?
I can't see anything.
Anything is possible.

vocabularies.definition: A thing that is not known or named.
vocabularies.example:
There is something in my eye.
I have something for you.
Something is wrong.

vocabularies.definition: Not anything; no single thing.
vocabularies.example:
There is nothing in the box.
I have nothing to say.
Nothing is free.

vocabularies.definition: All things in a particular situation.
vocabularies.example:
Everything is ready.
I like everything here.
Tell me everything.

vocabularies.definition: Any place; in, at, or to any place.
vocabularies.example:
I can't find it anywhere.
Go anywhere you like.
It's not anywhere here.

vocabularies.definition: In, at, or to a place that is not named.
vocabularies.example:
He lives somewhere in London.
Let's go somewhere quiet.
It must be somewhere.

vocabularies.definition: Not in, at, or to any place.
vocabularies.example:
There is nowhere to hide.
The path led to nowhere.
I have nowhere to go.

vocabularies.definition: In, at, or to every place.
vocabularies.example:
I've looked everywhere.
There were flowers everywhere.
Love is everywhere.

vocabularies.definition: Any person; anyone.
vocabularies.example:
Is there anybody there?
Anybody can join.
I don't know anybody.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is not named; someone.
vocabularies.example:
Somebody help me!
Somebody stole my bag.
I saw somebody in the garden.

vocabularies.definition: Not any person; no one.
vocabularies.example:
Nobody likes me.
There was nobody in the room.
Nobody knows the truth.

vocabularies.definition: All the people in a group; everyone.
vocabularies.example:
Everybody likes ice cream.
Is everybody ready?
Everybody knows that.

vocabularies.definition: Used for changing the subject or finishing a talk.
vocabularies.example:
I'll do it anyway.
It's too late anyway.
Anyway, let's go.

vocabularies.definition: In addition to someone or something else.
vocabularies.example:
What else do you need?
Someone else called.
Is there anything else?

vocabularies.definition: At any time; at any time in the past.
vocabularies.example:
Have you ever been to Paris?
Nothing ever happens.
Forever and ever.

vocabularies.definition: Not at any time; not once.
vocabularies.example:
I never drink coffee.
Never say never.
He never late.

vocabularies.definition: Many times or at regular intervals.
vocabularies.example:
I often go to the library.
How often do you swim?
We often meet at the park.

vocabularies.definition: Not often; rarely.
vocabularies.example:
He seldom watches TV.
It seldom rains in the desert.
We seldom see them.

vocabularies.definition: At all times; every time.
vocabularies.example:
Always brush your teeth.
I'll always love you.
You are always late!

vocabularies.definition: In the way that most often happens; normally.
vocabularies.example:
I usually wake up at six.
What do you usually do?
She usually wears a dress.

vocabularies.definition: Occasionally; some of the time.
vocabularies.example:
Sometimes I feel lonely.
Sometimes it snows in April.
Come and see us sometimes.

grammars.description: Cách dùng các đại từ bất định như Someone, Anything, Nowhere.
grammars.example:
Is there anyone home?
I have something for you.
